summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several specific developments in small UAS activity. Ukraine has unveiled the Gnome-NS, a new ground-based “mother drone” designed to carry FPVs, and also presented the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone. Russian forces have deployed a fiber-optic FPV drone in Kharkiv for the first time since the start of the full-scale invasion, and launched a major aerial attack ahead of Geneva talks with the US. In Zaporizhzhia, Ukrainian forces reportedly knocked out a Russian Kasta-2E2 radar using FPV drones, while a Russian FPV drone strike in Orikhiv injured a 67-year-old civilian. Ukraine’s $880 million drone line has reportedly turned Russian frontline movement into a “death sentence” in just 12 months, and Motor-G is now shipping 200,000 drone motors per month, making it Ukraine’s largest engine manufacturer.
